findings.message: `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` is already present in both `.env.example` files, so the outcome statement is inaccurate as written. The planned change is a wording/diagnostics update to existing entries, not adding a new variable.

The remaining test gap is still framed around a non-live path. The Reception mutation routes all call `requireStaffAuth()` and pass `auth.uid` into the Prime helper calls, so `actorUid` is not undefined on those route paths. If a route-level 401 surfacing test is wanted, it should target a real failure mode such as bad claims or secret mismatch, not `actorUid=undefined`.

The default assumption is misleading. `validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig()` is defined but not invoked anywhere in `apps/prime`, so there is no startup `console.warn` path to rely on today. Until a health/preflight hook is added, the only guaranteed signal is request-time failure/logging.
